description: A wind energy engineer is performing preliminary site screening for a prospective 20 MW wind farm in Wyoming. Model a 20 MW wind farm consisting of 10 × 2000 kW turbines in a 2x5 grid layout using PySAM's Windpower module and a Wyoming wind resource file (.srw) from the SAM installation (base path in /opt/SAM/sam_dir.txt). The configuration requires 10 turbines with 80 m hub height, 110 m rotor diameter, and a 2 MW power curve (cut-in 3-4 m/s, rated 12-13 m/s, cut-out 25 m/s) using the Simple (Park/Jensen) wake model. The 2-row × 5-column grid layout uses y-coordinates 0 and 770, and x-coordinates 0, 550, 1100, 1650, and 2200. Save the results to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/wind_farm_wake_analysis.json including the keys: farm_capacity_kw, annual_energy_kwh, capacity_factor_pct, wake_loss_pct, num_turbines, turbine_x_coords, turbine_y_coords, wind_resource_file, hub_height_m, and rotor_diameter_m.
